title: EU citizen residence registration
userSituation: I am an EU/EFTA/Swiss citizen staying in Poland and need to understand registration.
summary: Why EU/EFTA/Swiss citizens follow a registration procedure instead of a residence permit, what the certificate of registration is, and where the official pages are.
whoThisIsFor: EU, EFTA-EEA (Norway, Iceland, Liechtenstein), and Swiss citizens staying in Poland for more than 3 months
EU citizens confused about why they don't need a karta pobytu
People asked by a bank or employer for a "certificate of registration"
commonDocuments: Application for registration of residence of an EU citizen
Valid travel document or identity document
Documents connected to the purpose of stay (for example employment or study documents), as listed by official sources
Certificate of registration of residence (issued after registration)
whatThisExplains: Why this procedure is different from a non-EU temporary residence permit
That EU/EFTA/Swiss citizens staying over 3 months are expected to register their residence
That EU citizens can generally work in Poland without a work permit
Where the official registration information is
whatThisDoesNotDo: It does not assess your individual registration obligation
It does not prepare or review your application
It does not calculate the 3-month period for your case
It does not replace the official MOS and gov.pl pages linked below
body:
If you hold citizenship of an **EU country, Norway, Iceland, Liechtenstein, or
Switzerland**, your stay in Poland works differently from the residence-permit
system for non-EU citizens. You do not apply for a temporary residence permit
or a *karta pobytu*. Instead, official sources describe a **registration of
residence** (*rejestracja pobytu*) for stays longer than 3 months.

The registration is handled by the **voivodeship office** for the place where
you live, and official sources say the application is submitted in person.
After registration you can be issued a **certificate of registration of
residence of an EU citizen** — a document that banks, employers, and other
institutions often ask about. Official sources also note that not registering
can result in a fine, and that registration itself is free.

On work: official sources state that EU/EFTA/Swiss citizens generally have
**free access to the Polish labour market** and do not need a work permit.
The registration duty is about documenting a longer stay, not about work
authorization.

Documents mentioned in official sources depend on your purpose of stay — for
example employment documents for workers or enrolment documents for students.
The official pages below list the current requirements.
